req.title: Diagnostic report (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for DiagnosticReport → Observation linking requirement.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations;
    missing Observation links shall be rejected. Negative paths including
    permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state
    transitions must be tested.

Assumptions / conventions
--------------------------
* A thin service layer is tested through a well-defined interface:
      DiagnosticReportService  – business-logic facade
      ObservationRepository    – data store for observations
      AuthorizationService     – RBAC / permission checks
* FHIR R4 resource shapes are represented as plain Python dicts for
  portability; swap in your real model classes as needed.
* All external collaborators are replaced with pytest-mock / unittest.mock
  doubles so the suite has zero infrastructure dependencies.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from copy import deepcopy
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code (stubs – replace imports with your real module paths)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from diagnostic.services import DiagnosticReportService
# from diagnostic.repositories import ObservationRepository
# from diagnostic.auth import AuthorizationService
# from diagnostic.exceptions import (
#     AuthorizationError,
#     MissingObservationLinkError,
#     MissingMandatoryElementError,
#     InvalidStateTransitionError,
#     ObservationNotFoundError,
#     DuplicateReportError,
# )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inline stubs so the file is self-contained and runnable out of the box.
# Remove / replace when integrating with your actual codebase.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AuthorizationError(Exception):
    """Raised when the caller lacks required permissions."""


class MissingObservationLinkError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a DiagnosticReport contains no Observation references."""


class MissingMandatoryElementError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a required FHIR element is absent."""


class InvalidStateTransitionError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when a state transition is not permitted."""


class ObservationNotFoundError(LookupError):
    """Raised when a referenced Observation does not exist in the store."""


class DuplicateReportError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when an identical report already exists."""


class DiagnosticReportService:
    """Thin facade – real implementation lives in your service layer."""

    VALID_STATUSES = {"registered", "partial", "preliminary", "final",
                      "amended", "corrected", "appended", "cancelled",
                      "entered-in-error", "unknown"}

    # Permitted forward transitions: current_status → {allowed next statuses}
    ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[str, set[str]] = {
        "registered":        {"partial", "preliminary", "cancelled"},
        "partial":           {"preliminary", "final", "cancelled"},
        "preliminary":       {"final", "amended", "cancelled"},
        "final":             {"amended", "corrected", "appended",
                              "entered-in-error"},
        "amended":           {"corrected", "appended", "entered-in-error"},
        "corrected":         {"appended", "entered-in-error"},
        "appended":          {"entered-in-error"},
        "cancelled":         set(),
        "entered-in-error":  set(),
        "unknown":           set(),
    }

    def __init__(self, obs_repo, auth_service):
        self._obs_repo = obs_repo
        self._auth = auth_service
        self._store: dict[str, dict] = {}

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def issue(self, report: dict, issuer: dict) -> dict:
        """
        Validate and persist a new DiagnosticReport.

        Parameters
        ----------
        report  : FHIR-shaped DiagnosticReport dict
        issuer  : caller context (id, roles, …)

        Returns
        -------
        Persisted report dict (with server-assigned id & meta)

        Raises
        ------
        AuthorizationError              – caller not a clinician
        MissingMandatoryElementError    – required field absent
        MissingObservationLinkError     – result[] is empty or absent
        ObservationNotFoundError        – a referenced observation is unknown
        DuplicateReportError            – report.identifier already exists
        """
        # 1. Permission
        if not self._auth.can_issue_report(issuer):
            raise AuthorizationError(
                f"Caller {issuer.get('id')} lacks permission to issue reports."
            )

        # 2. Mandatory top-level elements
        for field in ("status", "code", "subject"):
            if not report.get(field):
                raise MissingMandatoryElementError(
                    f"DiagnosticReport missing mandatory field: '{field}'"
                )

        # 3. Observation links
        results = report.get("result", [])
        if not results:
            raise MissingObservationLinkError(
                "DiagnosticReport must reference at least one Observation "
                "via 'result'."
            )

        # 4. Each referenced observation must exist
        for ref in results:
            obs_id = ref.get("reference", "").replace("Observation/", "")
            if not self._obs_repo.exists(obs_id):
                raise ObservationNotFoundError(
                    f"Referenced Observation '{obs_id}' not found."
                )

        # 5. Duplicate check
        identifier = report.get("identifier")
        if identifier and self._store.get(identifier):
            raise DuplicateReportError(
                f"A report with identifier '{identifier}' already exists."
            )

        # 6. Persist
        persisted = deepcopy(report)
        persisted["id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
        persisted["meta"] = {
            "versionId": "1",
            "lastUpdated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        if identifier:
            self._store[identifier] = persisted
        return persisted

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def update_status(
        self,
        report_id: str,
        new_status: str,
        issuer: dict,
    ) -> dict:
        """
        Transition an existing report to *new_status*.

        Raises
        ------
        AuthorizationError          – caller lacks permission
        ObservationNotFoundError    – report not found
        InvalidStateTransitionError – transition not allowed
        MissingMandatoryElementError– new_status not a valid FHIR status
        """
        if not self._auth.can_update_report(issuer):
            raise AuthorizationError(
                f"Caller {issuer.get('id')} cannot update reports."
            )

        # Locate by scanning stored reports (demo only; use a real index)
        report = next(
            (r for r in self._store.values() if r.get("id") == report_id),
            None,
        )
        if report is None:
            raise ObservationNotFoundError(
                f"DiagnosticReport '{report_id}' not found."
            )

        if new_status not in self.VALID_STATUSES:
            raise MissingMandatoryElementError(
                f"'{new_status}' is not a valid FHIR DiagnosticReport status."
            )

        current = report["status"]
        if new_status not in self.ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(current, set()):
            raise InvalidStateTransitionError(
                f"Cannot transition DiagnosticReport from "
                f"'{current}' to '{new_status}'."
            )

        report["status"] = new_status
        report["meta"]["versionId"] = str(
            int(report["meta"]["versionId"]) + 1
        )
        return deepcopy(report)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def obs_repo():
    """Mock ObservationRepository whose .exists() returns True by default."""
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.exists.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def auth_service():
    """Mock AuthorizationService that grants all permissions by default."""
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.can_issue_report.return_value = True
    svc.can_update_report.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def service(obs_repo, auth_service):
    """DiagnosticReportService pre-wired with mock collaborators."""
    return DiagnosticReportService(
        obs_repo=obs_repo,
        auth_service=auth_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def clinician():
    """Authorised clinician caller context."""
    return {"id": "clinician-001", "roles": ["clinician"]}


@pytest.fixture()
def admin_user():
    """Administrative user – not a clinician."""
    return {"id": "admin-001", "roles": ["admin"]}


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_report():
    """Minimal valid DiagnosticReport referencing one Observation."""
    return {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "identifier": f"report-{uuid.uuid4()}",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/patient-123"},
        "result": [
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-001"}
        ],
    }


@pytest.fixture()
def issued_report(service, clinician, valid_report):
    """
    A report that has already been persisted.
    Returns (service, persisted_report) so tests can access the server id.
    """
    persisted = service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)
    return service, persisted


# ===========================================================================
# POSITIVE PATH – happy path tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestHappyPath:
    """Verify normal, successful workflows."""

    def test_issue_report_returns_persisted_document(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        result = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert result["id"], "Persisted report must have a server-assigned id"
        assert result["status"] == "preliminary"
        assert result["result"] == valid_report["result"]

    def test_issued_report_receives_meta_block(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        result = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert "meta" in result
        assert result["meta"]["versionId"] == "1"
        assert "lastUpdated" in result["meta"]

    def test_issue_report_with_multiple_observations(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = [
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-001"},
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-002"},
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-003"},
        ]
        result = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert len(result["result"]) == 3

    def test_status_transition_preliminary_to_final(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        updated = service.update_status(persisted["id"], "final", clinician)

        assert updated["status"] == "final"
        assert updated["meta"]["versionId"] == "2"

    def test_status_transition_increments_version(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)
        service.update_status(persisted["id"], "final", clinician)
        updated = service.update_status(persisted["id"], "amended", clinician)

        assert updated["meta"]["versionId"] == "3"

    def test_obs_repo_exists_called_for_each_reference(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, obs_repo
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = [
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-A"},
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-B"},
        ]
        service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        calls = [c.args[0] for c in obs_repo.exists.call_args_list]
        assert "obs-A" in calls
        assert "obs-B" in calls


# ===========================================================================
# NEGATIVE PATH – permission denial
# ===========================================================================

class TestPermissionDenial:
    """Callers without the clinician role must be rejected."""

    def test_non_clinician_cannot_issue_report(
        self, service, admin_user, valid_report, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.can_issue_report.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(AuthorizationError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, admin_user)

        assert "lacks permission" in str(exc_info.value).lower()

    def test_anonymous_caller_cannot_issue_report(
        self, service, valid_report, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.can_issue_report.return_value = False
        anonymous = {"id": None, "roles": []}

        with pytest.raises(AuthorizationError):
            service.issue(valid_report, anonymous)

    def test_non_clinician_cannot_update_status(
        self, service, clinician, admin_user, valid_report, auth_service
    ):
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        # Now revoke update permission
        auth_service.can_update_report.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(AuthorizationError):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "final", admin_user)

    def test_permission_check_is_called_exactly_once_on_issue(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, auth_service
    ):
        service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        auth_service.can_issue_report.assert_called_once_with(clinician)

    def test_permission_check_receives_correct_issuer_context(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, auth_service
    ):
        service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        auth_service.can_issue_report.assert_called_with(clinician)

    def test_authorisation_error_raised_before_validation(
        self, service, auth_service
    ):
        """Permission must be checked before field validation."""
        auth_service.can_issue_report.return_value = False

        # Report is intentionally incomplete
        with pytest.raises(AuthorizationError):
            service.issue({}, {"id": "hacker", "roles": []})


# ===========================================================================
# NEGATIVE PATH – missing Observation links
# ===========================================================================

class TestMissingObservationLinks:
    """result[] absent or empty must be rejected."""

    def test_report_without_result_field_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        del valid_report["result"]

        with pytest.raises(MissingObservationLinkError):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_report_with_empty_result_list_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = []

        with pytest.raises(MissingObservationLinkError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert "observation" in str(exc_info.value).lower()

    def test_error_message_mentions_result_field(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = []

        with pytest.raises(MissingObservationLinkError, match="result"):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_report_with_none_result_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = None

        with pytest.raises(MissingObservationLinkError):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_referenced_observation_does_not_exist_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, obs_repo
    ):
        obs_repo.exists.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(ObservationNotFoundError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert "obs-001" in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_partial_missing_observation_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, obs_repo
    ):
        """First obs exists; second does not – must still raise."""
        valid_report["result"] = [
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-exists"},
            {"reference": "Observation/obs-missing"},
        ]
        obs_repo.exists.side_effect = lambda obs_id: obs_id == "obs-exists"

        with pytest.raises(ObservationNotFoundError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert "obs-missing" in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_missing_observation_error_is_not_authorisation_error(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, obs_repo
    ):
        obs_repo.exists.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(ObservationNotFoundError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert not isinstance(exc_info.value, AuthorizationError)


# ===========================================================================
# NEGATIVE PATH – missing mandatory elements
# ===========================================================================

class TestMissingMandatoryElements:
    """status, code, and subject are mandatory; absence must be rejected."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["status", "code", "subject"])
    def test_report_missing_mandatory_field_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, missing_field
    ):
        del valid_report[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError) as exc_info:
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert missing_field in str(exc_info.value)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_value", [None, "", {}, []])
    def test_report_with_falsy_status_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, empty_value
    ):
        valid_report["status"] = empty_value

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_completely_empty_report_raises_mandatory_error(
        self, service, clinician
    ):
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, AuthorizationError)):
            service.issue({}, clinician)

    def test_mandatory_error_identifies_first_missing_field(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        del valid_report["status"]  # 'status' is checked first

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="status"):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_invalid_status_value_on_update_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="not a valid"):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "not-a-real-status", clinician)


# ===========================================================================
# NEGATIVE PATH – conflicting state transitions
# ===========================================================================

class TestConflictingStateTransitions:
    """Illegal status progressions must be rejected."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("terminal_status", ["cancelled", "entered-in-error"])
    def test_transition_from_terminal_state_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, terminal_status
    ):
        valid_report["status"] = "registered"
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        # Move to terminal state
        service.update_status(persisted["id"], terminal_status, clinician)

        # Any further transition should be rejected
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "preliminary", clinician)

    def test_cannot_regress_from_final_to_preliminary(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)
        service.update_status(persisted["id"], "final", clinician)

        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError) as exc_info:
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "preliminary", clinician)

        assert "final" in str(exc_info.value)
        assert "preliminary" in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_cannot_transition_from_registered_to_final_directly(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["status"] = "registered"
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "final", clinician)

    def test_same_status_transition_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        """Transitioning to the current status is not a valid progression."""
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)  # status=preliminary

        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], "preliminary", clinician)

    def test_update_nonexistent_report_raises_not_found(
        self, service, clinician
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ObservationNotFoundError):
            service.update_status("ghost-report-id", "final", clinician)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_transition", [
        ("final", "registered"),
        ("final", "partial"),
        ("amended", "preliminary"),
        ("corrected", "partial"),
        ("appended", "final"),
    ])
    def test_illegal_backward_transitions_are_all_rejected(
        self,
        service,
        clinician,
        valid_report,
        bad_transition,
    ):
        from_status, to_status = bad_transition
        valid_report["status"] = "preliminary"
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        # Walk forward to the from_status via valid path where possible
        path_to_from = {
            "final":     ["final"],
            "amended":   ["final", "amended"],
            "corrected": ["final", "amended", "corrected"],
            "appended":  ["final", "amended", "corrected", "appended"],
        }
        for step in path_to_from.get(from_status, [from_status]):
            try:
                service.update_status(persisted["id"], step, clinician)
            except InvalidStateTransitionError:
                pytest.skip(f"Cannot reach {from_status} via this path.")

        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(persisted["id"], to_status, clinician)


# ===========================================================================
# NEGATIVE PATH – duplicate report
# ===========================================================================

class TestDuplicateReport:
    """Submitting an identical identifier twice must be rejected."""

    def test_duplicate_identifier_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)

        with pytest.raises(DuplicateReportError):
            service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)

    def test_unique_identifiers_are_both_accepted(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        report_a = deepcopy(valid_report)
        report_a["identifier"] = "unique-id-A"
        report_b = deepcopy(valid_report)
        report_b["identifier"] = "unique-id-B"

        result_a = service.issue(report_a, clinician)
        result_b = service.issue(report_b, clinician)

        assert result_a["id"] != result_b["id"]

    def test_report_without_identifier_is_not_duplicate_checked(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        """Reports lacking an identifier bypass duplicate detection."""
        del valid_report["identifier"]

        # Both issues should succeed without a DuplicateReportError
        first = service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)
        second = service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)

        assert first["id"] != second["id"]


# ===========================================================================
# EDGE CASES & BOUNDARY CONDITIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:
    """Boundary conditions and unusual but plausible inputs."""

    def test_very_large_result_list_is_accepted(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = [
            {"reference": f"Observation/obs-{i}"} for i in range(500)
        ]
        result = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        assert len(result["result"]) == 500

    def test_result_entry_without_reference_key_is_rejected(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["result"] = [{"display": "No reference here"}]

        # Should either raise ObservationNotFoundError (empty reference
        # resolves to unknown obs) or MissingObservationLinkError
        with pytest.raises((ObservationNotFoundError, MissingObservationLinkError)):
            service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

    def test_obs_repo_receives_stripped_observation_id(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report, obs_repo
    ):
        """The 'Observation/' prefix must be stripped before lookup."""
        service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        obs_repo.exists.assert_called_with("obs-001")

    def test_issued_report_does_not_mutate_input(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        original_id = valid_report.get("id")
        service.issue(valid_report, clinician)

        # Service must not back-fill the id into the caller's dict
        assert valid_report.get("id") == original_id

    def test_concurrent_duplicate_submissions_raise_for_second(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        """Simulate rapid re-submission of the same report."""
        service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)

        # Immediate second attempt
        with pytest.raises(DuplicateReportError):
            service.issue(deepcopy(valid_report), clinician)

    def test_full_lifecycle_registered_to_entered_in_error(
        self, service, clinician, valid_report
    ):
        valid_report["status"] = "registered"
        persisted = service.issue(valid_report, clinician)
        report_id = persisted["id"]

        service.update_status(report_id, "partial", clinician)
        service.update_status(report_id, "preliminary", clinician)
        service.update_status(report_id, "final", clinician)
        service.update_status(report_id, "amended", clinician)
        final = service.update_status(report_id, "entered-in-error", clinician)

        assert final["status"] == "entered-in-error"
        assert final["meta"]["versionId"] == "6"
```

---

## Design Decisions

| Concern | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Isolation** | All external collaborators (`ObservationRepository`, `AuthorizationService`) are replaced with `MagicMock` doubles – zero infrastructure required |
| **Self-contained** | Inline stubs for the production classes mean the file runs with only `pytest` and the standard library |
| **Parametrisation** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize` covers all mandatory fields and all illegal backward transitions without code duplication |
| **Separation of concerns** | Five test classes mirror the five failure domains in the requirement (happy path, permissions, missing links, missing elements, bad transitions) |
| **Specificity** | Each assertion verifies the *exact* exception type and, where appropriate, that the message names the offending field or status |
| **No test interdependence** | Every test receives fresh fixtures; state is never shared between tests |

```python
"""
Pytest test suite for DiagnosticReport → Observation linking requirement.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations;
    missing Observation links shall be rejected. Negative paths including
    permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state
    transitions must be tested.

Assumptions / conventions
--------------------------
* A thin service layer is tested through a well-defined interface:
      DiagnosticReportService  – business-logic facade
      ObservationRepository    – data store for observations
      AuthorizationService     – RBAC / permission checks
* FHIR R4 resource shapes are represented as plain Python dicts for
  portability; swap in your real model classes as needed.
* All external collaborators are replaced with pytest-mock / unittest.mock
  doubles so the suite has zero infrastructure dependencies.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from copy import deepcopy
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code (stubs – replace imports with your real module paths)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# from diagnostic.repositories import ObservationRepository
# from diagnostic.auth import AuthorizationService

#     AuthorizationError,
#     MissingObservationLinkError,
#     MissingMandatoryElementError,

#     DuplicateReportError,
# )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inline stubs so the file is self-contained and runnable out of the box.

Pytest test suite for DiagnosticReport → Observation linking requirement.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations;
    missing Observation links shall be rejected. Negative paths including
    permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state
    transitions must be tested.

--------------------------
* A thin service layer is tested through a well-defined interface:

      ObservationRepository    – data store for observations

  doubles so the suite has zero infrastructure dependencies.

class AuthorizationError(Exception):
    """Raised when the caller lacks required permissions."""


class MissingObservationLinkError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a DiagnosticReport contains no Observation references."""


class MissingMandatoryElementError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a required FHIR element is absent."""


class InvalidStateTransitionError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when a state transition is not permitted."""


class ObservationNotFoundError(LookupError):
    """Raised when a referenced Observation does not exist in the store."""


class DuplicateReportError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when an identical report already exists."""

                      "amended", "corrected", "appended", "cancelled",
                      "entered-in-error", "unknown"}

    ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[str, set[str]] = {

        "partial":           {"preliminary", "final", "cancelled"},
        "preliminary":       {"final", "amended", "cancelled"},
        "final":             {"amended", "corrected", "appended",
                              "entered-in-error"},
        "amended":           {"corrected", "appended", "entered-in-error"},
        "corrected":         {"appended", "entered-in-error"},
        "appended":          {"entered-in-error"},
        "cancelled":         set(),
        "entered-in-error":  set(),
        "unknown":           set(),
    }
